brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is Ehrhart theory: enumerating integer points in the r-th dilate of a convex polytope (whose vertices are alternating sign matrices) to obtain a counting polynomial in r, tied to spin-r/2 vertex-model configurations. Paper B's machinery is difference-algebra symbolic summation, where the non-existence of a (parameterized) creative-telescoping solution certifies algebraic independence and transcendence of sums. The only overlap is the generic word 'sums/enumeration'; polytope lattice-point counting and telescoping-based independence proofs are unrelated processes with no role-for-role correspondence, so no coherent mapping can be filled in.
